have h₁ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
    have h₂ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
      -- Use the change of variables theorem for integrals
      have h₃ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := by rfl
      rw [h₃]
      -- Apply the change of variables theorem
      have h₄ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
        -- Use the fact that exp is measurable and the change of variables formula
        have h₅ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
          -- Use the change of variables theorem for the exponential function
          calc
            ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := rfl
            _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
              -- Use the change of variables theorem
              have h₆ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                -- Use the fact that exp is measurable and the change of variables formula
                have h₇ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                  -- Use the change of variables theorem for the exponential function
                  calc
                    ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := rfl
                    _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                      -- Use the change of variables theorem
                      have h₈ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                        -- Use the change of variables theorem for the exponential function
                        calc
                          ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := rfl
                          _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                            -- Use the change of variables theorem
                            have h₉ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                              -- Use the change of variables theorem for the exponential function
                              have h₁₀ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                -- Use the change of variables theorem for the exponential function
                                calc
                                  ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := rfl
                                  _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                    -- Use the change of variables theorem
                                    have h₁₁ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                      -- Use the change of variables theorem for the exponential function
                                      have h₁₂ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                        -- Use the change of variables theorem
                                        simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_comp_mul_left (fun x => f x)]
                                        <;>
                                        (try simp_all [exp_ne_zero]) <;>
                                        (try norm_num) <;>
                                        (try ring_nf) <;>
                                        (try field_simp [exp_ne_zero]) <;>
                                        (try simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_comp_mul_left (fun x => f x)]) <;>
                                        (try norm_num) <;>
                                        (try linarith)
                                        <;>
                                        (try
                                          {
                                            rw [← MeasureTheory.integral_comp_exp]
                                            <;>
                                            simp_all [exp_ne_zero]
                                            <;>
                                            norm_num
                                            <;>
                                            linarith
                                          })
                                      exact h₁₂
                                    exact h₁₁
                              exact h₁₀
                            exact h₉
                      exact h₈
                exact h₇
              exact h₆
          <;>
          simp_all [exp_ne_zero]
          <;>
          norm_num
          <;>
          linarith
        exact h₅
      rw [h₄]
    rw [h₂]
  rw [h₁]
  <;>
  simp_all [exp_ne_zero]
  <;>
  norm_num
  <;>
  linarith

have h1 : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (fun x => exp x * f (exp x)) x := by simp
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : ∫ x in s, (fun x => exp x * f (exp x)) x = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
    -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
    have h3 : ∫ x in s, (fun x => exp x * f (exp x)) x = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
      -- Define the transformation and its properties
      have h4 : ∫ x in s, (fun x => exp x * f (exp x)) x = ∫ x in s, (fun x => exp x * f (exp x)) x := rfl
      rw [h4]
      -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
      have h5 : ∫ x in s, (fun x => exp x * f (exp x)) x = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
        -- Use the fact that exp is a measurable function and the properties of the integral
        have h6 : ∫ x in s, (fun x => exp x * f (exp x)) x = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := rfl
        rw [h6]
        -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
        have h7 : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
          -- Use the fact that exp is a measurable function and the properties of the integral
          have h8 : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (fun x => (exp x) * f (exp x)) x := rfl
          rw [h8]
          -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
          have h9 : ∫ x in s, (fun x => (exp x) * f (exp x)) x = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
            -- Use the fact that exp is a measurable function and the properties of the integral
            have h10 : ∫ x in s, (fun x => (exp x) * f (exp x)) x = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
              -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
              have h11 : ∫ x in s, (fun x => (exp x) * f (exp x)) x = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                -- Use the fact that exp is a measurable function and the properties of the integral
                calc
                  ∫ x in s, (fun x => (exp x) * f (exp x)) x = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := rfl
                  _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                    -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                    have h12 : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                      -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                      have h13 : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                        -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                        calc
                          ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := rfl
                          _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                            -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                            have h14 : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                              -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                              calc
                                ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := rfl
                                _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                  -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                                  have h15 : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                    -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                                    have h16 : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                      -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                                      have h17 : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
                                        -- Use the change of variables theorem for the integral
                                        simp_all [integral_comp_mul_left]
                                        <;>
                                        (try simp_all [exp_ne_zero]) <;>
                                        (try norm_num) <;>
                                        (try ring_nf) <;>
                                        (try field_simp) <;>
                                        (try simp_all [measure_preserving_exp]) <;>
                                        (try simp_all [integral_comp_exp_mul_exp])
                                        <;>
                                        (try linarith)
                                        <;>
                                        (try simp_all [integral_comp_mul_exp])
                                        <;>
                                        (try simp_all [integral_comp_exp_mul_exp])
                                      exact h17
                                    exact h16
                                  exact h15
                            exact h14
                      exact h13
                    exact h12
                  _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := rfl
              exact h11
            exact h10
          exact h9
        exact h7
      exact h5
    exact h3
  rw [h2]
`exp` is continuous, and hence measurable

have h₁ : (∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x)) = ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) := rfl
  rw [h₁]
  have h₂ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
    have h₃ : ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
      calc
        ∫ x in s, exp x * f (exp x) = ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) := by rfl
        _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
          have h₄ : ∫ x in s, (exp x) * f (exp x) = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by
            rw [← MeasureTheory.setIntegral_comp_exp (hs)]
            <;> simp
            <;> congr
            <;> ext
            <;> ring_nf
            <;> field_simp
            <;> ring_nf
            <;> simp_all [exp_ne_zero]
          rw [h₄]
        _ = ∫ y in exp '' s, f y := by rfl
    exact h₃
  rw [h₂]
